title: How to enable the use of image layer cache while using imagebuildah go sdk
body:
**Description** When I am using [imagebuildah](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/containers/buildah/imagebuildah)-pkg to build multiple images using Dockerfiles. Images are able to build successfully but layers build cache are not being used while building images. I have set *[define.BuildOptions.Layers](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/containers/buildah@v1.32.0/define#BuildOptions)=true* **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Dockerfile: `test/Dockerfile` ```dockerfile FROM docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest RUN apt update RUN apt install nano -y ``` 2. Code: `main.go` ```go package main import ( "context" "fmt" "github.com/containers/buildah" "github.com/containers/buildah/define" "github.com/containers/buildah/imagebuildah" "github.com/containers/storage" "github.com/containers/storage/pkg/unshare" ) func main() { if buildah.InitReexec() { return } unshare.MaybeReexecUsingUserNamespace(false) // buildStore buildStoreOptions, err := storage.DefaultStoreOptionsAutoDetectUID() if err != nil { panic(err) } buildStore, err := storage.GetStore(buildStoreOptions) if err != nil { panic(err) } defer buildStore.Shutdown(false) buildOpts := define.BuildOptions{ Output: "new-test-image", Layers: true, SignaturePolicyPath: "conf/policy.json", } id, ref, err := imagebuildah.BuildDockerfiles(context.TODO(), buildStore, buildOpts, "test/Dockerfile") if err != nil { panic(err) } fmt.Printf("id: %+v\n", id) fmt.Printf("ref: %+v\n", ref) } ``` 3. Run: ```bash go run main.go ``` 4. SignaturePolicyPath: `conf/policy.json`
hidden { "default": [ { "type": "insecureAcceptAnything" } ], "transports": { "docker-daemon": { "": [{"type":"insecureAcceptAnything"}] } } }
**Describe the results you received:** The image was built successfully but didn't use cache for intermediate layers on rebuild. **Describe the results you expected:** The goal is to use `imagebuildah-pkg` to build Containerfile/Dockerfile while it uses the layer cache from previous builds. Same as what we would get using the CLI command: `buildah build --layers -t test:latest . ` **Output of `buildah version`:** ``` Version: 1.23.1 Go Version: go1.17 Image Spec: 1.0.1 Runtime Spec: 1.0.2-dev CNI Spec: 0.4.0 libcni Version: image Version: 5.16.0 Git Commit: Built: Thu Jan 1 05:30:00 1970 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 BuildPlatform: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `cat /etc/*release`:** ``` DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu DISTRIB_RELEASE=22.04 DISTRIB_CODENAME=jammy DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 22.04.3 LTS" PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 22.04.3 LTS" NAME="Ubuntu" VERSION_ID="22.04" VERSION="22.04.3 LTS (Jammy Jellyfish)" VERSION_CODENAME=jammy ID=ubuntu ID_LIKE=debian HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/" SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/" BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/" PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy" UBUNTU_CODENAME=jammy ``` **Output of `uname -a`:** ``` Linux 6.2.0-32-generic #32~22.04.1-Ubuntu SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri Aug 18 10:40:13 UTC 2 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux ```
